title: Collection Persistence, Versioned Schemas, Atomic Replacement, and Restore · Runtime Contract: Pokedex Go Load Validation
summary: The program writes live maps directly to the final file, trusts arbitrary JSON on load, has no schema version, loses permissions on replacement, and cannot recover from interruption. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go load validation increment in versioned atomic offline collection store. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Bound bytes, decode exactly one document, validate schema and every record, reject duplicates, and preserve the last valid in-memory state on error.
Define version, application identity, timestamp policy, records, provenance, and checksum or integrity scope with deterministic encoding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
